profiles.address: Buckingham Palace, London SW1A 1AA, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: The eldest child of Elizabeth II and Philip, Charles spent decades as Prince of Wales before acceding in 2022. His public identity blends constitutional monarchy, interfaith outreach, heritage crafts, environmental advocacy, and the long-running Prince's Trust legacy.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Known as Harry, he served in the British Army and founded the Invictus Games. Since stepping back from working royal life, his public story has centered family independence, media scrutiny, memoir, and mental-health advocacy.

profiles.profession: Olympic equestrian and retired Army officer
profiles.about_me: Anne's first husband won Olympic gold in eventing, served with the Queen's Dragoon Guards, and remained a prominent figure in equestrian sport after the marriage ended.
Anne's second husband served as an equerry to the Queen before a senior naval career. He is often seen beside the Princess Royal at ceremonial and charitable engagements.

The Queen's second son served as a Royal Navy helicopter pilot, including in the Falklands War. His later public life became defined by controversy, loss of royal patronages, and withdrawal from official duties.
Known widely as Fergie, Sarah remained closely associated with the York family after divorce. She writes children's books, works with charities, and retains a lively, media-friendly public persona.

profiles.profession: Duke of Edinburgh and royal patron
profiles.about_me: The youngest child of Elizabeth II and Philip supports youth, arts, sport, and the Duke of Edinburgh's Award. His work often has a quieter institutional style than the senior Wales and Sussex branches.

profiles.profession: Duchess of Edinburgh and charity advocate
profiles.about_me: A former public relations executive, Sophie is closely associated with work on preventable blindness, women's rights in conflict, disability inclusion, and a steady round of royal engagements.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: The heir apparent balances royal duties with causes around homelessness, emergency services, mental health, conservation, and the Earthshot Prize. He and Catherine anchor the younger working-royal branch.

profiles.place_of_birth: Park House, Sandringham, Norfolk, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Diana, Princess of Wales, became one of the most recognizable public figures of the late twentieth century. In this demo family she is represented as Charles's spouse and the mother of William and Harry, with her humanitarian work, media-era celebrity, and enduring public memory forming a major branch of the Windsor story.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Second in line after his father, George appears mostly at family and national events. His profile is intentionally protected while still marking the visible future of the monarchy.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Charlotte is the first British princess whose place in succession was not displaced by a younger brother under the modern succession rules. Public appearances often show her confident, observant role among the Wales children.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: The youngest Wales child is known through carefully limited public moments, including balcony appearances and family services, bringing an energetic youngest-child presence to the direct line.

Born into the Greek and Danish royal families, Philip served in the Royal Navy during the Second World War and became a restless modernizer of palace life, youth programs, conservation causes, and the Duke of Edinburgh's Award.

Catherine brought a non-aristocratic family background into the direct line of succession. Her public work emphasizes early childhood, mental health, visual storytelling, sport, and carefully staged family continuity.
The Sussexes' elder child was born in London and is being raised largely in California. His title usage changed after Charles III became King, making him a prince by male-line descent.
Named for Queen Elizabeth II's family nickname and Diana, Princess of Wales, Lilibet represents the California-born branch of the King's grandchildren.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Princess Anne's elder child has no royal title, reflecting his parents' preference. He has worked in sports, events, and sponsorship roles while remaining visible at family occasions.

profiles.profession: Queen of the United Kingdom and Commonwealth realms
profiles.about_me: Britain's longest-reigning monarch, Elizabeth II became Queen in 1952 and shaped the modern royal household through war memory, Commonwealth travel, constitutional restraint, jubilees, and a public style built on continuity and duty.

A former actor and lifestyle entrepreneur, Meghan joined the royal family in 2018. Her public narrative includes advocacy for women, media pressure, transatlantic identity, and building an independent household with Harry.

The King's only sister is known for a famously direct manner, a heavy working schedule, Olympic equestrian credentials, and long service with Save the Children, maritime, military, and farming organizations.
Born in Canada, Autumn married Peter Phillips in 2008 and became part of the untitled Phillips branch. The couple later divorced while co-parenting their daughters.

profiles.place_of_birth: St Mary's Hospital, London, United Kingdom
profiles.about_me: Princess Anne's daughter inherited the family equestrian streak, winning world and Olympic medals. Untitled by design, she is one of the family's sport-centered public figures.
A Rugby World Cup winner, Mike brought a relaxed sporting celebrity energy into the wider royal family and is often seen at equestrian and family events with Zara.
Peter and Autumn's younger daughter is part of Princess Anne's branch and generally kept away from heavy public exposure.
The first great-grandchild of Elizabeth II, Savannah appears occasionally at family events and belongs to the untitled, more private Phillips line.
Zara and Mike's second child continues the Queen's great-grandchild generation in the untitled Tindall branch.
The youngest Tindall child was born at home at Gatcombe Park, adding another cousin branch for the youngest Windsor generation.
The elder York sister works in business while supporting dyslexia, education, and charitable causes. Her wedding in 2020 reused Queen Mary's fringe tiara, linking her branch to royal history.
An Italian-British property developer, Edoardo married Princess Beatrice in 2020 and connects the York branch to the Mapelli Mozzi family.
Princess Beatrice and Edoardo's first daughter adds a young York-line cousin below the King's generation.
Princess Beatrice and Edoardo's younger daughter, born in 2025, helps exercise infant profiles and current-generation cousin logic.
The younger York sister works in the art world and supports anti-trafficking and scoliosis-related causes. Her public image mixes royal ceremony with a comparatively modern professional life.
A hospitality and drinks executive, Jack married Princess Eugenie at Windsor in 2018 and is part of the York branch's younger family life.
Eugenie and Jack's elder son carries names honoring Prince Albert and both family lines, giving the York branch another young cousin node.
Eugenie and Jack's younger son is another recent great-grandchild-generation profile for testing sibling and cousin grouping.
The elder child of Edward and Sophie grew up mostly outside the spotlight, is associated with carriage driving, and represents the Edinburgh branch's older child generation.
The eldest Tindall child sits in the extended, sporty Gatcombe branch and is usually seen only in family or equestrian contexts.
Edward and Sophie's younger child uses the courtesy title Earl of Wessex after his father became Duke of Edinburgh, creating another teenage cousin branch.
